description: Schema fragment (menu_ip.json). Merged into src/schema.json by src/schema/build.py. Do not load directly -- cross-fragment $ref uses '#/definitions/...' against the bundled output.

menu_ip.description: /ip -- top-level IPv4 menu. Houses addresses, ARP, DHCP client/server, DNS, firewall, services, routes, pools, and more. Known sub-menus get typed validation; unknown ones fall back to `menu_generic`.

pool.description: /ip/pool -- named IPv4 address pools (referenced by DHCP server).

menu_ip_dhcp_server.description: /ip/dhcp-server -- DHCP server instances. Either a plain list of server rows, or an object with `_items` plus `network` (per-subnet options) and `lease` (static reservations) sub-lists.

network.description: /ip/dhcp-server/network -- per-subnet options handed to clients (gateway, DNS, NTP, ...).

menu_ip_dns.description: /ip/dns -- DNS resolver / cache singleton. Either a plain list (with the singleton row), or an object with `_items` plus the `static` sub-list of static DNS records.

static.description: /ip/dns/static -- static DNS records (A, AAAA, CNAME, FWD, MX, NS, SRV, TXT, ...).

menu_ip_neighbor.description: /ip/neighbor -- LLDP/CDP/MNDP neighbour discovery. Holds the `discovery-settings` singleton sub-menu.

discovery-settings.description: /ip/neighbor/discovery-settings -- singleton policy for which interfaces participate in discovery, what TLVs to advertise, etc.

operation.description: `add` creates a new address row; `set` updates the row matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.ip.address.lan`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"LAN gateway"`.

address.description: Local address with CIDR prefix. Example: `192.168.88.1/24`.

network.description: Network address of the subnet (usually auto-derived). Example: `192.168.88.0`.

interface.description: Interface (by `iac.*` token) the address is bound to. Example: `iac.bridge.lan`.

broadcast.description: Broadcast address of the subnet (usually auto-derived). Example: `192.168.88.255`.

netmask.description: Dotted-quad netmask alternative to CIDR in `address`. Example: `255.255.255.0`.

disabled.description: If true, the address is created with `disabled=yes`. Example: `false`.

operation.description: `add` creates a new DHCP client row; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.ip.dhcp-client.wan`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"ISP uplink"`.

interface.description: Interface (by `iac.*` token) on which to run DHCP. Example: `iac.ether.wan`.

name.description: Display name of the dhcp-client row (RouterOS 7+). Example: `wan`.

add-default-route.description: Install a default route from the lease. Accepts `yes`/`no`/`special-classless`. Example: `yes`.

default-route-distance.description: Administrative distance of the installed default route. Example: `1`.

default-route-tables.description: Comma-separated routing tables to install the default route into. Example: `main`.

dhcp-options.description: Comma list of DHCP options to request. Example: `hostname,clientid`.

use-peer-dns.description: Apply DNS servers handed out by the DHCP server. Example: `true`.

use-peer-ntp.description: Apply NTP servers handed out by the DHCP server. Example: `false`.

script.description: RouterOS script run on lease events. Example: `:log info "got lease"`.

disabled.description: If true, the client is created with `disabled=yes`. Example: `false`.

item_ip_pool.description: /ip/pool -- named pool of IPv4 addresses for DHCP.

operation.description: `add` creates a new pool; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.ip.pool.lan`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"LAN DHCP pool"`.

next-pool.description: Pool to fall over to when this one is exhausted (or `none`). Example: `none`.

item_ip_dhcp_server.description: /ip/dhcp-server -- a DHCP server instance bound to an interface + pool.

operation.description: `add` creates a new DHCP server; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.dhcp.lan`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"LAN DHCP"`.

name.description: Server name (referenced from network/lease rows). Example: `iac.dhcp.lan`.

interface.description: Interface the server listens on. Example: `iac.bridge.lan`.

address-pool.description: Pool name to lease from (or `static-only`). Example: `iac.pool.lan`.

authoritative.description: NAK clients with leases from other servers. Example: `yes`.

delay-threshold.description: Delay processing requests with elapsed-time above the threshold. Example: `none`.

always-broadcast.description: Always reply to broadcast (workaround for some clients). Example: `false`.

add-arp.description: Add an ARP entry for each lease. Example: `true`.

client-mac-limit.description: Max leases per client MAC. Example: `unlimited`.

allow-dual-stack-queue.description: Share a simple queue with the IPv6 lease. Example: `false`.

conflict-detection.description: Send ICMP echo before handing out an address. Example: `true`.

lease-script.description: Script run on lease events (assigned/deassigned). Example: omit.

relay.description: Address of the relay agent (when not on the same subnet). Example: `0.0.0.0`.

src-address.description: Source address used for replies. Example: `192.168.88.1`.

disabled.description: If true, the server is disabled. Example: `false`.

item_ip_dhcp_server_network.description: /ip/dhcp-server/network -- network options handed out to clients (gateway, DNS, ...).

operation.description: `add` registers a network; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.dhcp.network.lan`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"LAN options"`.

address.description: Subnet to which these options apply. Example: `192.168.88.0/24`.

gateway.description: Default gateway handed to clients. Example: `192.168.88.1`.

dns-server.description: Comma list of DNS servers handed to clients. Example: `192.168.88.1`.

ntp-server.description: Comma list of NTP servers handed to clients. Example: `192.168.88.1`.

wins-server.description: Comma list of WINS servers handed to clients. Example: omit.

domain.description: Search domain handed to clients. Example: `lan`.

caps-manager.description: CAPsMAN address advertised in option. Example: omit.

dhcp-option.description: Comma list of named DHCP options. Example: `tftp-server`.

dhcp-option-set.description: Named option-set to attach. Example: omit.

operation.description: `add` creates a static lease; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.dhcp.lease.printer`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"office printer"`.

address.description: Pinned address for the client. Example: `192.168.88.10`.

mac-address.description: Client MAC the lease is keyed on. Example: `AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF`.

server.description: DHCP server (by `iac.*` token) the lease belongs to. Example: `iac.dhcp.lan`.

lease-time.description: Per-lease override of the server default. Example: `1d`.

address-lists.description: Comma list of address-lists to add the leased IP to. Example: `iac.addr.known`.

always-broadcast.description: Always reply to broadcast for this client. Example: `false`.

block-access.description: Reject this client (keep the row for tracking). Example: `false`.

use-src-mac.description: Match on Ethernet source MAC instead of option 61. Example: `true`.

dhcp-option.description: Comma list of named DHCP options. Example: omit.

dhcp-option-set.description: Named option-set to attach. Example: omit.

disabled.description: If true, the lease is disabled. Example: `false`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.ip.dns`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"DoH primary"`.

allow-remote-requests.description: Serve DNS to clients (turn the router into a resolver). Example: `true`.

dynamic-servers.description: Read-only -- resolvers learned via DHCP/PPP. Example: omit.

cache-max-ttl.description: Upper bound on cached record TTL. Example: `1d`.

max-concurrent-tcp-sessions.description: Limit on TCP sessions to upstream. Example: `20`.

max-udp-packet-size.description: Maximum UDP DNS response size. Example: `4096`.

query-total-timeout.description: Overall query timeout across servers. Example: `10s`.

verify-doh-cert.description: Verify the DoH server's TLS certificate. Example: `true`.

doh-max-concurrent-queries.description: Limit on in-flight DoH queries. Example: `50`.

doh-max-server-connections.description: Connection pool size to the DoH server. Example: `5`.

address-list-extra-time.description: Extra TTL added to dynamic address-list entries from DNS. Example: `10m`.

mdns-repeat-ifaces.description: Comma list of interfaces to bridge mDNS traffic across. Example: `bridge-lan,bridge-iot`.

operation.description: `add` creates a new record; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.dns.static.router`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"pin gateway"`.

name.description: FQDN matched by this record. Example: `router.lan`.

regexp.description: Regex matched against query names (alternative to `name`). Example: `.*\.lan$`.

srv-weight.description: SRV weight for load-balancing at equal priority. Example: `100`.

ttl.description: Cache TTL handed to clients. Example: `1h`.

address-list.description: Address-list to add the answer IP to. Example: `iac.addr.allow`.

disabled.description: If true, the record is created with `disabled=yes`. Example: `false`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.ip.neighbor`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"hide from WAN"`.

discover-interface-list.description: Interface-list whose members may receive/send discovery (CDP/LLDP/MNDP). Example: `iac.list.mgmt`.

lldp-mac-phy-config.description: Advertise MAC/PHY config TLV in LLDP. Example: `false`.

lldp-max-frame-size.description: Advertise max-frame-size TLV in LLDP. Example: `false`.

lldp-med-net-policy-vlan.description: LLDP-MED voice VLAN to advertise. Example: `disabled`.

lldp-poe-power.description: Advertise PoE power TLV in LLDP. Example: `false`.

lldp-vlan-info.description: Advertise VLAN TLV in LLDP. Example: `false`.

protocol.description: Comma list of enabled discovery protocols. Example: `lldp,mndp`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.ip.service.ssh`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"mgmt only"`.

address.description: Comma list of CIDRs permitted to access the service. Example: `10.0.0.0/24`.

port.description: TCP port the service listens on. Example: `2222`.

certificate.description: Certificate name for SSL services. Example: `router-cert`.

disabled.description: If true, the service is disabled. Example: `true`.

item_ip_firewall_address_list.description: /ip/firewall/address-list -- one entry in a named address-list.

operation.description: `add` appends a new entry; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

filter.description: Find expression for `set`. Example: `[find list=blocklist address=1.2.3.4]`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.addr.lan-clients`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"office subnet"`.

list.description: Address-list name the entry belongs to. Example: `lan_clients`.

address.description: IPv4 address, CIDR, range, or comma list. Example: `192.168.88.0/24`.

disabled.description: If true, the entry is created with `disabled=yes`. Example: `false`.

operation.description: `add` appends a rule; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

filter.description: Find expression for `set`. Example: `[find chain=srcnat comment~"masq"]`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.nat.masq.wan`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"masquerade WAN"`.

src-address-list.description: Match if source IP is on this list. Example: `iac.addr.lan`.

dst-address-list.description: Match if destination IP is on this list. Example: `iac.addr.public`.

port.description: Match either src- or dst-port. Example: `53`.

protocol.description: IP protocol name or number. Example: `tcp`.

in-interface-list.description: Match ingress on any iface in this list. Example: `iac.list.lan`.

out-interface-list.description: Match egress on any iface in this list. Example: `iac.list.wan`.

log-prefix.description: String prefixed to log lines. Example: `nat-masq`.

address-list-timeout.description: Expiry for entries added by the action. Example: `1h`.

connection-mark.description: Match a connection mark set earlier in mangle. Example: `vpn`.

src-mac-address.description: Match a source MAC address. Example: `AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF`.

disabled.description: If true, the rule is created with `disabled=yes`. Example: `false`.

operation.description: `add` appends a rule; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

filter.description: Find expression for `set`. Example: `[find chain=input comment~"icmp"]`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.fw.input.icmp`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"allow ICMP"`.

src-address-list.description: Match if source IP is on this list. Example: `iac.addr.lan`.

dst-address-list.description: Match if destination IP is on this list. Example: `iac.addr.blocked`.

port.description: Match either src- or dst-port. Example: `53`.

protocol.description: IP protocol name or number. Example: `tcp`.

in-interface-list.description: Match ingress on any iface in this list. Example: `iac.list.lan`.

out-interface-list.description: Match egress on any iface in this list. Example: `iac.list.wan`.

in-bridge-port-list.description: Match ingress on any bridge port in this list. Example: `iac.list.lan-ports`.

out-bridge-port-list.description: Match egress on any bridge port in this list. Example: `iac.list.lan-ports`.

layer7-protocol.description: Named L7 regex profile to match. Example: `omit`.

log-prefix.description: String prefixed to log lines. Example: `fw-drop`.

p2p.description: Match a P2P protocol. Example: omit.

packet-size.description: Packet size range in bytes. Example: `64-1500`.

random.description: Match a random N percent of packets. Example: `50`.

src-mac-address.description: Match a source MAC address. Example: `AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF`.

address-list-timeout.description: Expiry for entries added by the action. Example: `1d`.

disabled.description: If true, the rule is created with `disabled=yes`. Example: `false`.
